behavior_summary: When the player inspects or "looks at" this item/NPC, the script displays a single visible bark/text line: "Flame Sting."
Immediately after emitting the bark, the U8P_FLAMSTNG::look process yields/terminates (the code issues a suspend), so no further interaction or branching occurs.
This script functions as a simple U8P_FLAMSTNG::look/label handler tied to an in-game item (likely a special weapon) rather than a dialogue-capable NPC.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FLAMSTNG::look displays one Bark node containing "Flame Sting" and then issues a suspend/terminate — effectively a one-shot display followed by process end (high confidence).
open_questions: Is the displayed text "Flame Sting" intended as the item's in-world name/label or a short descriptive flavor string? (inferred; confidence: medium)

Is this script attached to an equippable weapon instance with the referenced item properties (baseDamage, damageModifier, types, attackDexBonus, isSpecial), or are those properties only present in a separate item definition file? (inferred; confidence: low)
Are there any engine-side side effects when this U8P_FLAMSTNG::look process suspends (e.g., analytics, focus restoration) that are not visible in the script? (unknown; confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_FLAMSTNG::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short/visible text when the player examines the object — show "Flame Sting" and then end the U8P_FLAMSTNG::look interaction.
entry_conditions: Player performs a 'U8P_FLAMSTNG::look' / inspect action on the object or NPC.

function_map.name: U8P_FLAMSTNG::look
function_map.role: U8P_FLAMSTNG::look/inspect handler that emits a single bark text then suspends the process
